title: Redirect Revalidation, DNS, Address Classes, Proxies, and SSRF · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds connection-time SSRF defense record from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of connection-time SSRF defense record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Allowlist schemes and ports, reject embedded credentials, and prohibit non-HTTP destinations before resolution.
Classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Resolve every Location and reapply authorization, scope, credential, method, DNS, loop, and budget rules per hop.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scheme Port Credenti…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9698 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to block unsafe schemes, addresses, redirects, rebinding, proxies, and metadata while retaining causal evidence; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scheme Port Credenti….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allowlist schemes and ports, reject embedded credentials, and prohibit non-http destinations before resolution.
